calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

outcome.source_quote: 3337 dogs vaccinated in Lalitpur, Kirtipur, Lumbini, Nepalgunj and Mahagadhimai - Routine vaccination of dogs is the most effective way of preventing rabies outbreaks in the community, thus protecting both animals and humans. In 2022, Animal Nepal successfully vaccinated over 3300 dogs against Rabies in different parts of Nepal.

outcome.source_quote: 1892 dogs treated in Kathmandu valley & Lumbini - Through our dog treatment centers in Chobhar, Lumbini and our Mobile Treatment Service for Lalitpur & Kirtipur, in 2022 Animal Nepal was able to provide vital medical treatment for over 1800 dogs suffering with various injuries & illnesses.

outcome.source_quote: 38 cats sterilized and 42 cats treated - Animal Nepal runs its Animal Birth Control program for cats in partnership with Catmandu Lovers, supported by Help Animals India. Due to the shy and solitary nature of cats, they are not as easy to catch as dogs but every cat that we are able to sterilize, makes a difference to the cat's welfare and helps to control the issue of cat overpopulation in Nepal. Animal Nepal also provides mobile treatment service for cats that are injured and sick and in 2022, we were able to help 42 cats regain their health.

calculated_metrics.name: Impact Equivalency Score (IES)

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: No valid counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 31%, Companion Animals: 69%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

beneficiaries.location: Lalitpur, Kirtipur, Mahagadhimai, Lumbini and Nepalgunj

source.quote: 1968 dogs sterilized in Lalitpur, Kirtipur, Mahagadhimai, Lumbini and Nepalgunj - Animal Birth Control continues to be one of our priorities with the work we do for companion animals. In 2022, thanks to the support of our partners Help Animals India, Brigitte Bardot Foundation, Dogs Trust Worldwide, Marchig Animal Welfare Trust, Nepalgunj Sub-Metropolitan City, Lumbini Cultural Municipality & Lumbini Development Trust, we were able to successfully sterilize close to 2000 dogs in different parts of the country.

1892 dogs treated in Kathmandu valley & Lumbini - Through our dog treatment centers in Chobhar, Lumbini and our Mobile Treatment Service for Lalitpur & Kirtipur, in 2022 Animal Nepal was able to provide vital medical treatment for over 1800 dogs suffering with various injuries & illnesses.

beneficiaries.location: Lalitpur, Kirtipur, Lumbini, Nepalgunj and Mahagadhimai

source.quote: 3337 dogs vaccinated in Lalitpur, Kirtipur, Lumbini, Nepalgunj and Mahagadhimai - Routine vaccination of dogs is the most effective way of preventing rabies outbreaks in the community, thus protecting both animals and humans. In 2022, Animal Nepal successfully vaccinated over 3300 dogs against Rabies in different parts of Nepal.
24 dogs adopted - In 2022, 24 of Animal Nepal's rescued puppies and dogs were adopted into loving homes including sisters Joon and Tara (pictured above).
38 cats sterilized and 42 cats treated - Animal Nepal runs its Animal Birth Control program for cats in partnership with Catmandu Lovers, supported by Help Animals India.

38 cats sterilized and 42 cats treated - Animal Nepal runs its Animal Birth Control program for cats in partnership with Catmandu Lovers, supported by Help Animals India. Due to the shy and solitary nature of cats, they are not as easy to catch as dogs but every cat that we are able to sterilize, makes a difference to the cat's welfare and helps to control the issue of cat overpopulation in Nepal. Animal Nepal also provides mobile treatment service for cats that are injured and sick and in 2022, we were able to help 42 cats regain their health.

3205 working equines helped in 2022: These equines were given direct health care by Animal Nepal in 2022 in the form of preventative healthcare such as deworming, vaccinations as well as treatment for injuries and sickness.

At the heart of Animal Nepal's mission lies a deep commitment to advocating for the well-being of all animals. We believe that every animal deserves to live a life free from suffering, cruelty and neglect. This core belief has fueled our determination to bring about lasting change, and I am incredibly proud of what we have accomplished over the years.
